---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}
body {
  font-family: 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif;
  background-color: #f7f9fb;
  color: #333333;
  line-height: 1.7;
  padding: 30px;
  max-width: 900px;
  margin: auto;
}

h2 {
  color: #2c7fb8;
  border-bottom: 2px solid #e0e0e0;
  padding-bottom: 5px;
}

p {
  margin-bottom: 12px;
}

img {
  border-radius: 10px;
  margin-top: 10px;
  margin-bottom: 10px;
  display: block;
  margin-left: auto;
  margin-right: auto;
}

pre {
  background-color: #f4f4f4;
  padding: 10px;
  border-radius: 6px;
}


```

## Community transport patterns across different time periods

This dynamic report explores transport patterns in my community using observational data collected at different times of the day. The data were collected in four time periods: morning, midday, afternoon, and evening.

<div style="text-align: center;">
  <img src="https://media1.tenor.com/m/a1oFXxkcOI8AAAAC/pusheen-pusheen-car.gif" width="400">
</div>

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vQoMXO1WqygkgxtGEPEfJvQ27P0SWWvSZjWvcqI1a7lMKNe1lwgmFpkcEuJFb3tE-buIRDUohX0_khI/pub?output=csv")

latest_data <- logged_data |>
  rename(
    time_period = `Time period`,
    transport_mode = `Transport mode`,
    travelling_group = `Travelling group`,
    direction = `Direction of movement`
  )

time_summary <- latest_data |> count(time_period)
transport_summary <- latest_data |> count(transport_mode)
group_summary <- latest_data |> count(travelling_group)
direction_summary <- latest_data |> count(direction)

```

There are currently `r nrow(latest_data)` observations in the dataset.

The busiest time period is `r time_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(time_period)`, with `r time_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(n)` observations.

The most common transport mode is `r transport_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(transport_mode)`.


```{r}
latest_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = transport_mode)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Transport mode distribution",
    x = "Transport mode",
    y = "Count"
  )

top_mode <- transport_summary |> 
  slice_max(n, n = 1) |> 
  pull(transport_mode)
```

This bar chart shows the overall distribution of transport modes in the sample.
The most common transport mode is `r transport_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(transport_mode)`, with `r transport_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(n)` observations.


```{r}
latest_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = transport_mode, fill = time_period)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Transport mode by time period",
    x = "Transport mode",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Time period"
  )

```

This chart compares transport modes across different time periods and shows that transport behaviour changes throughout the day.
The busiest time period is `r time_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(time_period)`, with `r time_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(n)` observations.


```{r}
latest_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = transport_mode, fill = travelling_group)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Transport mode by travelling group",
    x = "Transport mode",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Travelling group"
  )

```

This chart compares transport modes by travelling group and helps show whether people are more likely to travel alone or with others depending on transport type.
Most observations involve people travelling `r group_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(travelling_group)`.


## Summary

Overall, there are `r nrow(latest_data)` observations in this dataset.

The most common transport mode is `r transport_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(transport_mode)`, while the busiest time period is `r time_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(time_period)`.

Most people were observed travelling `r group_summary |> slice_max(n, n = 1) |> pull(travelling_group)`, suggesting that individual travel is more common in this community.

These patterns indicate that transport behaviour varies across the day and is influenced by convenience and daily routines.

<div style="text-align: center;">
  <img src="https://media1.tenor.com/m/lXfIGgqRzYYAAAAC/traffic-public-transport.gif" width="400">
</div>
